checks.detail: All required files present.
solution/solve.sh, oracle.yaml, and job.yaml all present.
All required fields present and within limits.
git and tmux installed via apt-get.
tests/ contains compute_reward.py in addition to test.sh; should live in environment/tests/.
reasons: compute_reward.py present in tests/ root

checks.detail: No stray non-ignored files at root.

notes: Move tests/compute_reward.py to environment/tests/compute_reward.py and update the Dockerfile to COPY it into /tests/, so the tests/ folder at task root contains only test.sh.

checks.detail: task.toml declares [agent] user = "agent".
All required isolation steps are present in the Dockerfile.
reasons: present: useradd agent
present: chown -R agent:agent /app
present: chown agent:agent /solution
present: mkdir /tests && chmod 700 /tests (re-applied after staging)
present: strace installed
present: root-owned reference inputs in /tests/refdata (chmod go-rwx)

checks.detail: test.sh locks /logs/verifier first then wipes, runs agent code via su agent under strace with timeout, scores outside strace, and always exits 0.
reasons: present: chmod 700 /logs/verifier BEFORE rm -rf
present: re-chmod 700 /tests after upload
present: env sanitisation (unset LD_PRELOAD, PYTHONPATH, etc.)
present: su agent -c 'env -i ...' inside strace -f for build + every link/run
present: timeout 60/120 wrapping strace
present: strace openat reward.(txt|json) write-flag detection
present: compute_reward.py invoked OUTSIDE strace via python3 -ISs
present: trap fallback writes reward.txt=0 on early exit
present: exit 0 at end and in fail_with
present: pkill -u agent before/after agent phase

checks.detail: runs_agent_code — the verifier builds and executes the agent's myld and the ELFs it produces.
Task has no external Python/Node/Rust dependencies; only apt packages pinned by base image and musl-tools.
No Python package manager used; task is C systems-programming with apt-installed toolchain only.
task.toml has no docker_image set; expected during development but must be set before final delivery.
reasons: docker_image not set in [environment]

notes: Set [environment].docker_image to a prebuilt registry image before final delivery.

checks.detail: Clearly states the bug is in relocation handling, the constraint to keep a single myld.c, the invocation interface, and that the executable must actually run correctly.
No explicit time bound or hint to keep best result updated during iteration.
reasons: No mention of sandbox timeout or iterative best-effort guidance.

checks.detail: Reads as a colleague handoff with debugging tips, not a formal spec.
57 lines, well under the 100-line cap.
No references to scoring, verifier, reward, tests in a grading sense, or evaluation pipeline.
Mentions make, readelf, objdump, musl-gcc, gcc, and points to musl install path and <elf.h>.

notes: Consider adding a one-line time hint (e.g., 'sandbox runs for ~2 hours; keep myld.c in a working state as you iterate').

Instruction says tests/ has 'pre-compiled .o files' but the visible workspace tests/ only contains source files; either pre-build them in the image or reword to 'source files you can compile' to avoid agent confusion.

checks.detail: Baseline myld.c is a stub that fails execution; structural checks are gated behind execution gates so noop yields 0.0.

Reward file locked (chmod 700 + wipe), /tests 0700, agent runs under su agent with env -i, strace catches reward writes and linker shellouts via execve regardless of source obfuscation, oracle marker requires unforgeable secret token.
All agent build/link/run uses `su agent -s /bin/bash -c "env -i ..."` inside strace.
strace -f wraps both make and each link/run job; logs grepped for reward file writes (=>reward_file_manipulation fail) and for execve of ld/gcc/etc (=>shellout flag).
compute_reward.py only reads /logs/verifier/test_results.json (root-only) and /app/myld.c as text; executed via python3 -ISs outside strace, never imports /app code.
EXIT trap guarantees reward.txt exists, fail_with always exit 0, every agent invocation wrapped in timeout (60s/120s), no `set -euo pipefail`.
Each test diffs agent ELF stdout+exit against a root-owned reference; structural checks gated behind actual execution pass so a non-running ELF scores ~0.
Tests score the produced ELF's runtime behavior (stdout/exit) vs a reference, not how relocations were implemented; any correct linker passes.
Weighted sum across tiers is fine because higher tiers strictly subsume lower-tier capabilities, so the agent cannot game an easy dimension while ignoring hard ones to claim full credit.
Banned-pattern + strace exec scan blocks shelling out to ld/gcc, structural-gate-on-execution blocks valid-but-broken ELFs, and exact diff against reference output blocks degenerate outputs.
compute_reward.py reads test_results.json and myld.c text only; no agent code is imported or executed and it can be re-run independently.
Both success and --fail paths emit reward.json with score, subscores list (one per tier, empty allowed semantics preserved), and additional_data, plus reward.txt.

Verifier runs the produced ELFs and diffs stdout/exit against reference binaries plus minimal readelf structural gates, matching the instruction's explicit goal that 'the executable must run correctly... actually produce the right output when executed'.

All required concepts (relocation types, PC-relative vs absolute, ELF object/archive handling, musl link order) are standard ELF/linker terminology and the musl crt1/crti/libc.a/crtn link order is shown verbatim in instruction.md.

Dockerfile installs build-essential, musl-tools, musl-dev, strace, tmux, git; workspace has myld.c (841 lines), Makefile, start.S, syscalls.S, and all referenced tests/ source files matching the verifier's tier set.
solution/solve.sh exists (152 lines), writes the HARBOR_ORACLE_FLAG marker and a wrapper myld.c that delegates to ld/gcc, then runs make.
Verifier links agent's myld against root-owned pristine copies of the exact .o/.a files visible to the agent in /app/tests; no new test inputs or undocumented requirements are introduced.
Slim base, cache cleaned, every installed package (build-essential, musl-tools, git, tmux, strace, procps) is used by the build or verifier.
solution/ contains only solve.sh.
Core defenses (lock+wipe /logs/verifier, /tests re-lock, strace+su agent, pkill, shellout detector) are all justified; minor env-unset overlap with env -i.
reasons: useful: chmod 700 + rm -rf /logs/verifier as first step
useful: pkill -u agent + survival probe before grading
useful: strace openat+execve scan with shellout sentinel feeding compute_reward
useful: env -i inside su agent strips inherited env for agent phase
redundant: top-level unset LD_PRELOAD/LD_LIBRARY_PATH/PYTHONPATH/... is largely shadowed by env -i in every su agent invocation

checks.detail: No files >30MB anywhere in the task.
workspace/ holds only the starter linker (myld.c, Makefile, start.S, syscalls.S) and test sources the instructions point to; no stale configs or dead files.

notes: Consider dropping the redundant top-level `unset` line in test.sh or moving compute_reward.py under environment/tests/ to match the documented tests-dir convention.

instruction_quality.task_name: Fix x86-64 ELF static linker relocations
instruction_quality.task_summary: An existing C implementation of a minimal x86-64 Linux static linker (myld.c) compiles and emits valid ELF but mis-handles relocations, causing cross-file calls and symbol references to misbehave. The agent must diagnose and fix the relocation logic so linked programs run and produce correct output, without shelling out to ld or gcc.
instruction_quality.deliverable: myld.c (fixed) producing a working ELF executable via `make`

measures: /tests chmod 700 root-owned
/tests/refdata chmod -R go-rwx (root-only .ref + pristine .o/.a)
/logs/verifier chmod 700 + rm -rf before any test
Reference binaries removed from /app/tests at build time
Agent user non-root, runs under su agent -c
env -i strips LD_PRELOAD/PYTHONPATH/CFLAGS/etc.
Staged .o/.a copies are root-owned 644 (agent reads but cannot mutate)
OUTDIR is the only agent-writable path during link
strace -f on make + every link/run job (build.log + per-case logs)
openat() of reward.txt|reward.json with O_WRONLY/O_RDWR/O_CREAT => fail with reward_file_manipulation
execve() of /ld|/gcc|/musl-gcc|/clang|/lld|/collect2|etc => shellout flag => 0 reward
pkill -9 -u agent before each phase to prevent cross-phase persistence
Source code substring scan for execvp/system/popen banned patterns
150 non-blank line minimum on /app/myld.c
Structural readelf checks gated behind genuine execution gates (no credit for syntactically valid but non-running ELFs)
Oracle bypass requires marker file containing the secret HARBOR_ORACLE_FLAG which agent never sees
test_results.json written only by root grading phase after strace fence
compute_reward.py runs python3 -ISs (isolated, no PYTHONPATH)
Atomic reward write (mkstemp + os.replace)
EXIT trap guarantees reward.txt fallback on any abort

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Source-substring banned-pattern scan misses exec families like execv/execl/posix_spawn
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Defended at runtime: strace -f records all execve() calls and grep flags any path ending in /(ld|gcc|musl-gcc|clang|lld|collect2|...) regardless of which libc wrapper produced it.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent can run benign helper binaries (as, objcopy, ar, ld-not-in-list)
acknowledged_vectors.reason: These cannot link an executable from .o inputs on their own; the strace pattern covers every standard linker driver path component.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent can read pristine staged .o/.a inputs in /tmp during link phase
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Intentional — agent needs to read inputs to link them. They are 644 root-owned so agent cannot mutate them, only consume.

acknowledged_vectors.vector: Agent can write to its own /logs/agent traces
acknowledged_vectors.reason: Documented in Harbor model as unavoidable; does not affect reward computation which reads only /logs/verifier/test_results.json.

reward_design.formula: score = clamp01( sum_over_tiers( weight_t * (passed_t / total_t) ) ) * anti_gaming_pass; weights: T1=0.08, T2=0.12, T3=0.16, T4=0.24, T5=0.40; structural sub-tests gated by their execution-pass counterpart
reward_design.formula_explanation: Tiered weighted sum rewards incremental linker capability (basic ELF -> cross-file -> bss/archive -> musl structural -> full musl programs) while a multiplicative anti-gaming gate zeroes the score on shellout or banned patterns.

components.name: T1 basic ELF
components.metric: diff vs reference stdout+exit on single-.o inline syscall + structural readelf gated by run
components.gate: structural sub-test only counted if execution sub-test passed

components.name: T2 cross-file
components.metric: diff vs reference for hello/data multi-object programs + structural gated
components.gate: structural sub-test only counted if execution sub-test passed

components.name: T3 bss + archive
components.metric: diff vs reference for .bss and .a-pulling programs

components.name: T4 structural + musl hello
components.metric: readelf entry/.text/no-dynsym checks + musl hello run-diff
components.gate: structural checks gated by t4_hello_libc execution pass

components.name: T5 complex musl
components.metric: diff vs reference for printf, multi-file musl, common symbols

components.name: anti-gaming gate
components.metric: strace shellout detection (ld/gcc/clang/lld/cc1/collect2/musl-gcc), banned source patterns (system/popen/execvp ld/gcc), min 150 non-blank lines, oracle marker bypass
components.gate: multiplicative: failing zeroes the whole reward

dimension_analysis.name: T1 basic ELF
dimension_analysis.notes: Already mostly works in baseline per instruction; small weight.

dimension_analysis.name: T2 cross-file relocations
dimension_analysis.notes: Core of the bug to fix (PC-rel relocation formula).

dimension_analysis.name: T3 bss + archive
dimension_analysis.notes: Requires archive member pull plus .bss/relocation correctness.

dimension_analysis.name: T4 musl structural+hello
dimension_analysis.notes: Structural checks alone are easy but gated by hello_libc execution.

dimension_analysis.name: T5 complex musl programs
dimension_analysis.notes: Highest weight; requires correct relocations across crt1+libc.a.

redundant_items.item: unset LD_PRELOAD LD_LIBRARY_PATH PYTHONPATH PYTHONHOME BASH_ENV ENV CFLAGS CC CXX MAKEFLAGS (line 52)
redundant_items.why: All agent execution paths already use `su agent -s /bin/bash -c "env -i PATH=... bash ..."`, which discards the parent environment; the top-level unset only affects root's own strace invocation where these vars are not consulted.

redundant_items.item: compute_reward.py lives in tests/ at the task root
redundant_items.why: Convention is that tests/ at the task root contains only test.sh; other test assets belong in environment/tests/ and get baked into the image. Functionally fine here (Harbor uploads it next to test.sh) but inconsistent with the documented layout.
